data.title: Strategist: The US-Iran conflict may end in May, rather than the previously expected June.
data.description: Strategists at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia believe the US-Iran conflict may end in May, rather than the previously expected June. While the ceasefire does not end the fighting, analysts see it as the beginning of an end. In a report, analysts stated that the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz indicates substantial progress towards a peace agreement. They added that although the plans proposed by the US and Iran appear seriously incompatible, reaching a ceasefire suggests that the US is tentatively open to compromise, given that Iran's strategic leverage has not significantly diminished and US allies remain unwilling to provide assistance. They added that the economic consequences of the war are continuing to accumulate, and if negotiations break down due to political constraints, the market will react strongly negatively. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Market Sentiment Stabilizes Amid Recent Price Rebound
data.description:
On April 8, on-chain analyst Murphy shared insights into the recent Bitcoin market trends. According to BlockBeats, Murphy noted that as Bitcoin rebounded to the $71,000–$72,000 range, the average realized profit over the past 24 hours was approximately $18.79 million, significantly lower than the profits seen in previous rebounds. Additionally, short-term holders experienced an average realized loss of about $6.06 million in the same period, which is considered low in recent times.

Overall, investor sentiment in the market is gradually stabilizing, showing a marked improvement from the extreme pessimism observed in February. During the price rebound, investors are not rushing to cash in on profits, nor are they eager to cut losses, indicating expectations for a higher rebound potential. For those holding short positions, it is advised not to be overly persistent. Despite indicators suggesting a need for correction due to overbought conditions and divergence, short-term opportunities remain limited, necessitating caution and avoiding prolonged engagements.

data.title: AI Platform Mentis Detects Unusual Market Signals Amid Geopolitical Developments
data.description:
Mentis, an AI agent trading platform, has recently observed unusual fluctuations in information flow and market sentiment related to the United States, Israel, and Iran. According to ChainCatcher, this was followed by a rapid shift in the Middle East towards ceasefire negotiations, drawing significant market attention due to concentrated geopolitical developments.

The platform's trading interface indicated that during the early stages of these events, Mentis AI Signal detected continuous buy and trend confirmation signals. Multiple model consensus systems, including MTS-GPT, MTS-DS, and MTS-Q Flash, simultaneously identified a strengthening market structure. Subsequently, Bitcoin's price quickly ascended, breaking through key levels, which validated the direction indicated by the AI signals.

Trading records reveal that the AI Agent executed automated trades and managed positions following signal formation, with account profits rising in tandem with market trends. This demonstrates the AI's ability to make forward-looking judgments during the early stages of event-driven signals.

Mentis states that its AI Agent continuously tracks cross-market information sources, sentiment changes, and event data, creating a closed-loop process from information discovery to signal analysis, decision support, and automated execution. This approach aims to enhance response efficiency during sudden macroeconomic and geopolitical events.

data.title: Spot silver rose 6.00% on the day, currently trading at $77.37 per ounce.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver rose 6.00% on the day, currently trading at $77.37 per ounce.

data.title: Machi Big Brother Increases Leveraged Positions in HYPE and Ethereum
data.description: Machi Big Brother has significantly increased his leveraged positions in the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, he opened a 10x leveraged long position in HYPE, bringing his total holdings to 28,888 tokens. Additionally, he continued to add to his 25x leveraged long position in Ethereum, which now totals 10,925 ETH. Hyperbot data indicates that his total portfolio is valued at approximately $25.651 million, with a floating profit of around $1.1 million.

data.title: Quantum Computing's Energy Demand to Out-Mine Bitcoin: A Significant Challenge
data.description: Researchers have highlighted the immense energy requirements for a quantum computer to surpass Bitcoin mining capabilities. According to NS3.AI, such a computer would need approximately 10^25 watts and around 10^23 qubits, equating to about 3% of the Sun's energy output. Meanwhile, separate academic studies have suggested that many reported breakthroughs in quantum factoring are based on simplified setups. These studies emphasize that older or reused Bitcoin addresses pose a more credible long-term risk.

data.title: OKX Star: The allegations against Li Lin are completely false. If whistleblowing could determine the outcome, this industry would have ceased to exist long ago.
data.description: OKX Star responded to the claim of "reporting Li Lin to the Chinese police" by stating: "This is completely false information. In the Asian cryptocurrency industry, any large platform and founder faces numerous reports and complaints every year. If reports could determine the outcome, this industry would have ceased to exist long ago. Huobi's CEO, Mr. Li, has high emotional intelligence and has managed the various people around him very well over the years. He shouldn't believe such nonsense that defies common sense."

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Rises 2% Against U.S. Dollar Amid Rate Hike Discussions
data.description: The New Zealand dollar (NZD) experienced a significant increase of 2% against the U.S. dollar (USD), currently trading at 0.5847. According to Jin10, this surge follows the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's announcement that it discussed the possibility of raising interest rates today. The discussions have sparked interest in the currency markets, as investors weigh the potential impact of a rate hike on the New Zealand economy. The central bank's considerations come amid ongoing evaluations of economic conditions and inflationary pressures.

data.title: CZ's early phone number and other personal information were mistakenly revealed in his new book.
data.description: In his memoir, *Binance Life*, CZ inadvertently leaked his early personal information, including his phone number and WeChat ID. Specifically, the book includes a screenshot of an old business card containing CZ's personal information, which was not redacted. For more interesting content from CZ's new book, *Binance Life*, please read "CZ's New Book Reveals Ten Secrets: Early Knowledge of 'September 4th,' First Public Revelation of Huobi's Ownership Change."

data.title: CZ: Swipe Offered 80% Equity for Binance Partnership, Later Sold Remainder for Millions
data.description: According to Odaily, Binance co-founder CZ mentioned early information about the "Binance payment card" in his personal autobiography "Binance Life." He stated: "In February 2020, I was introduced to the Philippine crypto startup Swipe Wallet. It was originally a wallet but later pivoted to offering crypto debit cards. Users pay with cryptocurrency, while merchants receive fiat currency, making the experience almost seamless for users.The idea itself wasn't new; the biggest challenge was finding banking partners. For crypto enthusiasts, it eliminated the hassle of selling crypto for fiat, allowing cryptocurrency to be used directly for daily payments. At that time, Swipe wasn't officially operational yet but wanted to gain users through a partnership with Binance. Founder Joseph offered to exchange 80% of Swipe's equity for a partnership. He was very smart and later sold the remaining 20% to us for tens of millions of dollars.Subsequently, Binance launched the Binance Card in Europe, which quickly became a favorite among users."

data.title: New Zealand PM Luxon: Fuel Supply Remains Stable Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Luxon stated that the government will continue to monitor potential risks to the country's economy, aiming to minimize impacts on inflation and economic growth. According to Jin10, the latest fuel update indicates that New Zealand's domestic fuel stocks are sufficient, with additional supplies en route. Fuel importers have not reported any issues with future orders or transportation. Consequently, the nation remains in the first stage of its national fuel response plan.

data.title: CZ: Voluntarily Pleaded Guilty in the US to Mitigate Impact on Binance Users and the Crypto Industry
data.description: According to Odaily, Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) stated in his new book "Binance Life" that in 2023, he voluntarily traveled to the United States and admitted to violating relevant provisions of the U.S. Bank Secrecy Act, primarily related to Binance providing services to U.S. users in its early stages without completing registration. CZ claimed that against the backdrop of tightening regulatory environment at the time, failing to make this decision could have brought greater uncertainty and potential shocks to Binance's operations, BNB holders, and industry development. CZ expressed that he chose to address the risks by personally assuming legal responsibility, aiming to reduce the impact on platform users and the overall cryptocurrency industry while ensuring the continued operation of the business.

data.title: Brother Machi's overall position turned from loss to profit and he opened another long position in HYPE with 10x leverage.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Huang Licheng (aka "Machi Dage") has opened another long position in HYPE with 10x leverage, currently holding 28,888 HYPE. In addition, he has continued to add small amounts to his long position in Ethereum with 25x leverage, currently holding 10,925 ETH, with a liquidation price of $2066. With the rebound in the crypto market, Machi Dage's overall position has turned from loss to profit, with a position value of approximately $25.651 million and a current unrealized profit of approximately $1.1 million.

data.title: Indian Rupee Appreciation Impact on Inflation and GDP, Central Bank Reports
data.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a monetary policy report indicating that a 5% appreciation of the Indian rupee against the benchmark exchange rate could lead to a decrease in inflation and GDP growth by 40 basis points and 25 basis points, respectively. According to Jin10, the report highlights the potential economic implications of currency fluctuations on the country's macroeconomic indicators. The RBI's analysis underscores the sensitivity of inflation and GDP growth to changes in the exchange rate, suggesting that a stronger rupee could have a dampening effect on both inflationary pressures and economic expansion.

data.title: India's Central Bank Holds Rates Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: On April 8, India's central bank announced its decision to maintain interest rates following the outbreak of the Middle East crisis. According to Jin10, the bank aims to support economic growth while addressing the significant depreciation of the rupee. The Monetary Policy Committee unanimously voted to keep the benchmark repo rate at 5.25%, aligning with market expectations. The policy stance remains neutral. This decision reflects the central bank's cautious approach amid tensions between Iran and the United States, which pose challenges to India's energy supply and growth outlook. Since the conflict began, the rupee's decline has been a major concern for the Reserve Bank of India, occupying much of its attention in recent days. Central Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that the committee maintains a neutral stance to retain the flexibility needed to effectively respond to new pressures.

data.title: Bank Indonesia: Future Rate Cut Space Narrowing Due to Global Developments
data.description: Bank Indonesia has indicated that the potential for future interest rate cuts is becoming limited due to evolving global conditions. According to Jin10, the central bank's assessment reflects concerns over international economic trends that may impact Indonesia's monetary policy decisions. The statement highlights the challenges faced by the central bank in balancing domestic economic needs with external pressures. This development comes as central banks worldwide navigate complex economic landscapes influenced by geopolitical tensions and market volatility.

data.title: EDU surged over 34% in a short period, currently trading at $0.058.
data.description: Market data shows that, possibly influenced by CZ's new book, EDU briefly surged by over 34%, breaking through $0.059, and is currently trading at $0.058.

data.title: USD1 Deposit Rate Surges to 35.81% Amid WLFI Transactions
data.description: The deposit rate for USD1 has unexpectedly surged to 35.81%, raising questions about the current financial situation. Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that over the past five days, the WLFI strategic reserve address has deposited 3 billion WLFI tokens into Dolomite, borrowing 50.44 million USD1. This activity has led to a complete depletion of USD1 on Dolomite, with the borrowing rate now at 30% and liquidity showing a deficit of 232,000 tokens. Potential investors are advised to consider the timing of USD1 withdrawals before seeking interest gains.

data.title: India's Central Bank Governor Projects CPI Inflation at 4.6% This Year
data.description: India's central bank governor has projected the country's Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation rate to be 4.6% for the current year. According to Jin10, this forecast reflects ongoing economic assessments and policy considerations by the Reserve Bank of India. The projection is part of the central bank's broader strategy to manage inflationary pressures and ensure economic stability. The governor's statement underscores the importance of monitoring inflation trends as India navigates its economic challenges.
